data.title: BYD (SEHK:1211) Valuation Check As P/E Premium Contrasts With Cash Flow Upside Signals
data.description:
Advertisement

Recent share performance and business scale

Without a clear single news catalyst, you might be looking at BYD (SEHK:1211) simply because its recent share performance and business size stand out among Hong Kong listed auto and battery groups.

Over the past month, BYD shares have returned about 5.9%, while the past 3 months show a modest 1.3% decline. On a 1 year view, the total return figure provided is 14.2%.

The company reports revenue of CN¥841.1b and net income of CN¥38.3b. This reflects a large-scale operation across automobiles, batteries and related services. Annual revenue growth is given as 10.8%, with net income growth at 16.3%.

At the last close of HK$102.80, some valuation metrics in the data suggest a potential gap between market price and certain fair value estimates. However, these approaches depend heavily on the assumptions behind each model.

See our latest analysis for BYD.

Recent trading points to rebuilding momentum, with a 1 day share price return of 4.6% and a 7 day share price return of 3.8%, set against a 1 year total shareholder return of 14.2% that reflects steadier long term gains.

If BYD has you thinking more broadly about the auto space, it could be a good time to see what else is on the move among auto manufacturers.

So with BYD trading at HK$102.80 alongside fair value and price target estimates that sit higher, is the current share price a genuine gap you can exploit, or is the market already factoring in future growth?

Price to earnings of 21.7x: Is it justified?

On the latest numbers, BYD trades on a P/E of 21.7x, while our fair value work points to a lower P/E of 13.8x and the shares sit below some cash flow based estimates such as the SWS DCF model fair value of HK$136 versus the last close at HK$102.80.

The P/E ratio compares the current share price with earnings per share and is a common way for investors to judge how much they are paying for each unit of profit. For a large auto and battery group like BYD, a higher P/E can reflect expectations for further earnings progress as well as the breadth of its businesses across vehicles, batteries and related services.

Here, the 21.7x P/E sits above both the Asian auto industry average of 18.2x and a peer average of 8.3x. This suggests the market is assigning BYD a richer earnings tag than many regional and peer names. Compared with the estimated fair P/E of 13.8x, the gap is also wide. This highlights a level that the market could move towards if sentiment or earnings expectations change.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for BYD

Result: Price to earnings of 21.7x (OVERVALUED)

However, you still need to watch for earnings expectations resetting if sentiment toward higher P/E auto names cools or if analyst price targets are revised lower.

Find out about the key risks to this BYD narrative.

Another view: Cash flow says something different

If you put the earnings multiple to one side and look at our SWS DCF model, you get a different message. On this view, BYD at HK$102.80 sits about 24.4% below an estimated fair value of HK$136, which points to potential upside rather than stretch.

That gap matters because it shows how sensitive conclusions can be to the tool you use, and to the assumptions built into it. If profits or cash flows play out differently, could this apparent cushion shrink or widen from here?

data.title: Northwest Bancshares (NWBI) Net Interest Margin Of 3.69% Reinforces Bullish Profitability Narratives
data.description:
Northwest Bancshares (NWBI) has put up a solid finish to FY 2025, with Q4 total revenue of US$172.2 million and basic EPS of US$0.31. The company also reported trailing 12 month revenue of US$599.1 million and EPS of US$0.93, alongside earnings growth of 25.8% over the last year. Over recent periods the company has seen total revenue move from US$498.1 million on a trailing basis in Q4 2024 to US$599.1 million in Q4 2025, while trailing EPS has gone from US$0.79 to US$0.93 as net margin held around 21%. This sets up a results season where profitability is front and center for investors.

See our full analysis for Northwest Bancshares.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed narratives around growth, risk, and income potential for Northwest Bancshares.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Loan book edges past US$13b with margin near 3.7%

  • Total loans reached about US$13.0b in Q4 2025, with a net interest margin of 3.69% and a cost to income ratio of 59.52%.
  • What stands out for a more optimistic view is that loan balances moved from roughly US$11.2b in Q1 2025 to about US$13.0b by Q4, while the trailing 12 month net profit margin sat at 21%. This gives bulls a story of a larger loan book still earning solid spreads.
    • Supporters pointing to earnings momentum can lean on trailing EPS of US$0.93 and net income of US$126.0m over the last year, alongside the 25.8% earnings growth figure that has already been reported.
    • At the same time, Q4 2025 net interest margin of 3.69% compares with 3.33% in Q3 2024, which fits a bullish argument that core banking profitability metrics have held up while scale has increased.
To see how that loan growth and margin profile fit into the bigger bullish and cautious stories around Northwest Bancshares, check out the latest narrative views on the company. 📊 Read the full Northwest Bancshares Consensus Narrative.

Non performing loans rise to about US$108m

  • Non performing loans moved from US$59.4m in Q1 2025 to US$107.9m in Q4 2025, after being US$62.1m in Q4 2024.
  • Critics focus on the more cautious angle that credit quality is a watch point, and the step up in non performing loans gives that view some backing, even alongside the reported 21% net margin.
    • Bears can point to the move from US$62.1m of non performing loans in Q4 2024 to US$107.9m in Q4 2025 as a sign that more loans are not currently paying as agreed, which they see as a risk to future earnings.
    • They may also link this with the large US$42.8m one off loss recorded in the last 12 months, arguing that both items remind investors that recent strong earnings growth figures can sit alongside credit and one time loss pressures.

Valuation gap vs DCF fair value and rich P/E

  • The current share price of US$12.76 sits well below the stated DCF fair value of about US$25.30, while the stock trades on a P/E of 14.8x compared with peers at 12.9x and the US Banks industry at 11.8x.
  • What is interesting for you as an investor is that the same data that suggests a large gap to DCF fair value also shows Northwest Bancshares on a richer P/E than peers, so bullish and bearish takes both find support in the numbers.
    • Supporters often highlight that the share price is roughly 49.6% below the provided DCF fair value of US$25.30, and combine that with 25.8% trailing earnings growth and a 6.27% dividend yield to argue there is room for value and income at the same time.
    • Skeptics instead point to the 14.8x P/E relative to 12.9x for peers and 11.8x for the broader US Banks group, saying that even with the valuation gap to DCF fair value, the market is already paying more per dollar of trailing earnings than for many similar banks.

Next Steps

data.title: Sopharma AD's (BUL:SFA) five-year total shareholder returns outpace the underlying earnings growth
data.description:
The most you can lose on any stock (assuming you don't use leverage) is 100% of your money. But on the bright side, if you buy shares in a high quality company at the right price, you can gain well over 100%. One great example is Sopharma AD (BUL:SFA) which saw its share price drive 223% higher over five years. It's also good to see the share price up 33% over the last quarter. But this move may well have been assisted by the reasonably buoyant market (up 17% in 90 days).

While this past week has detracted from the company's five-year return, let's look at the recent trends of the underlying business and see if the gains have been in alignment.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

While the efficient markets hypothesis continues to be taught by some, it has been proven that markets are over-reactive dynamic systems, and investors are not always rational. One way to examine how market sentiment has changed over time is to look at the interaction between a company's share price and its earnings per share (EPS).

Over half a decade, Sopharma AD managed to grow its earnings per share at 4.1% a year. This EPS growth is slower than the share price growth of 26% per year, over the same period. So it's fair to assume the market has a higher opinion of the business than it did five years ago. And that's hardly shocking given the track record of growth.

The graphic below depicts how EPS has changed over time (unveil the exact values by clicking on the image).

This free interactive report on Sopharma AD's earnings, revenue and cash flow is a great place to start, if you want to investigate the stock further.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

When looking at investment returns, it is important to consider the difference between total shareholder return (TSR) and share price return. The TSR is a return calculation that accounts for the value of cash dividends (assuming that any dividend received was reinvested) and the calculated value of any discounted capital raisings and spin-offs. Arguably, the TSR gives a more comprehensive picture of the return generated by a stock. In the case of Sopharma AD, it has a TSR of 339% for the last 5 years. That exceeds its share price return that we previously mentioned. This is largely a result of its dividend payments!

A Different Perspective

It's nice to see that Sopharma AD shareholders have received a total shareholder return of 99% over the last year. And that does include the dividend. Since the one-year TSR is better than the five-year TSR (the latter coming in at 34% per year), it would seem that the stock's performance has improved in recent times. Someone with an optimistic perspective could view the recent improvement in TSR as indicating that the business itself is getting better with time. I find it very interesting to look at share price over the long term as a proxy for business performance. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. data.title: Mennica Polska (WSE:MNC) Seems To Use Debt Quite Sensibly
data.description:
David Iben put it well when he said, 'Volatility is not a risk we care about. What we care about is avoiding the permanent loss of capital.' So it seems the smart money knows that debt - which is usually involved in bankruptcies - is a very important factor, when you assess how risky a company is. As with many other companies Mennica Polska S.A. (WSE:MNC) makes use of debt. But should shareholders be worried about its use of debt?

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Why Does Debt Bring Risk?

Generally speaking, debt only becomes a real problem when a company can't easily pay it off, either by raising capital or with its own cash flow. Part and parcel of capitalism is the process of 'creative destruction' where failed businesses are mercilessly liquidated by their bankers. While that is not too common, we often do see indebted companies permanently diluting shareholders because lenders force them to raise capital at a distressed price. Of course, plenty of companies use debt to fund growth, without any negative consequences. The first thing to do when considering how much debt a business uses is to look at its cash and debt together.

What Is Mennica Polska's Net Debt?

The image below, which you can click on for greater detail, shows that at September 2025 Mennica Polska had debt of zł277.9m, up from zł306.0k in one year. However, because it has a cash reserve of zł106.9m, its net debt is less, at about zł171.1m.

How Strong Is Mennica Polska's Balance Sheet?

We can see from the most recent balance sheet that Mennica Polska had liabilities of zł1.08b falling due within a year, and liabilities of zł196.3m due beyond that. Offsetting this, it had zł106.9m in cash and zł139.6m in receivables that were due within 12 months. So its liabilities outweigh the sum of its cash and (near-term) receivables by zł1.03b.

This deficit isn't so bad because Mennica Polska is worth zł2.57b, and thus could probably raise enough capital to shore up its balance sheet, if the need arose. But we definitely want to keep our eyes open to indications that its debt is bringing too much risk.

See our latest analysis for Mennica Polska

In order to size up a company's debt relative to its earnings, we calculate its net debt divided by its earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) and its earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) divided by its interest expense (its interest cover). This way, we consider both the absolute quantum of the debt, as well as the interest rates paid on it.

We'd say that Mennica Polska's moderate net debt to EBITDA ratio ( being 1.5), indicates prudence when it comes to debt. And its commanding EBIT of 1k times its interest expense, implies the debt load is as light as a peacock feather. It is just as well that Mennica Polska's load is not too heavy, because its EBIT was down 44% over the last year. When a company sees its earnings tank, it can sometimes find its relationships with its lenders turn sour. When analysing debt levels, the balance sheet is the obvious place to start. But you can't view debt in total isolation; since Mennica Polska will need earnings to service that debt. So if you're keen to discover more about its earnings, it might be worth checking out this graph of its long term earnings trend.

Finally, a company can only pay off debt with cold hard cash, not accounting profits. So we always check how much of that EBIT is translated into free cash flow. Over the last three years, Mennica Polska actually produced more free cash flow than EBIT. That sort of strong cash generation warms our hearts like a puppy in a bumblebee suit.

Our View

Mennica Polska's EBIT growth rate was a real negative on this analysis, although the other factors we considered were considerably better. In particular, we are dazzled with its interest cover. When we consider all the factors mentioned above, we do feel a bit cautious about Mennica Polska's use of debt. While debt does have its upside in higher potential returns, we think shareholders should definitely consider how debt levels might make the stock more risky. There's no doubt that we learn most about debt from the balance sheet. However, not all investment risk resides within the balance sheet - far from it. To that end, you should be aware of the 1 warning sign we've spotted with Mennica Polska .

At the end of the day, it's often better to focus on companies that are free from net debt. You can access our special list of such companies (all with a track record of profit growth). It's free.

data.title: The past year for Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles (NSE:TMPV) investors has not been profitable
data.description:
Investors can approximate the average market return by buying an index fund. While individual stocks can be big winners, plenty more fail to generate satisfactory returns. That downside risk was realized by Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Limited (NSE:TMPV) shareholders over the last year, as the share price declined 53%. That's disappointing when you consider the market returned 7.9%. Longer term shareholders haven't suffered as badly, since the stock is down a comparatively less painful 25% in three years. The falls have accelerated recently, with the share price down 17% in the last three months.

With that in mind, it's worth seeing if the company's underlying fundamentals have been the driver of long term performance, or if there are some discrepancies.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

To paraphrase Benjamin Graham: Over the short term the market is a voting machine, but over the long term it's a weighing machine. By comparing earnings per share (EPS) and share price changes over time, we can get a feel for how investor attitudes to a company have morphed over time.

Unfortunately Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles reported an EPS drop of 69% for the last year. The share price fall of 53% isn't as bad as the reduction in earnings per share. So the market may not be too worried about the EPS figure, at the moment -- or it may have expected earnings to drop faster.

The graphic below depicts how EPS has changed over time (unveil the exact values by clicking on the image).

Before buying or selling a stock, we always recommend a close examination of historic growth trends, available here.

Advertisement

What About Dividends?

When looking at investment returns, it is important to consider the difference between total shareholder return (TSR) and share price return. Whereas the share price return only reflects the change in the share price, the TSR includes the value of dividends (assuming they were reinvested) and the benefit of any discounted capital raising or spin-off. It's fair to say that the TSR gives a more complete picture for stocks that pay a dividend. In the case of Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles, it has a TSR of -4.3% for the last 1 year. That exceeds its share price return that we previously mentioned. The dividends paid by the company have thusly boosted the total shareholder return.

A Different Perspective

Investors in Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles had a tough year, with a total loss of 4.3% (including dividends), against a market gain of about 7.9%. However, keep in mind that even the best stocks will sometimes underperform the market over a twelve month period. Longer term investors wouldn't be so upset, since they would have made 17%, each year, over five years. If the fundamental data continues to indicate long term sustainable growth, the current sell-off could be an opportunity worth considering. I find it very interesting to look at share price over the long term as a proxy for business performance. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. data.title: Strong week for PBG (BVMF:PTBL3) shareholders doesn't alleviate pain of five-year loss

The share price has failed to impress anyone , down a sizable 62% during that time. So we're not so sure if the recent bounce should be celebrated. However, in the best case scenario (far from fait accompli), this improved performance might be sustained.

The recent uptick of 14% could be a positive sign of things to come, so let's take a look at historical fundamentals.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

PBG wasn't profitable in the last twelve months, it is unlikely we'll see a strong correlation between its share price and its earnings per share (EPS). Arguably revenue is our next best option. Its history of dividend payouts mean that PBG's TSR, which was a 55% drop over the last 5 years, was not as bad as the share price return.

data.title: Is Centene (CNC) Pricing Reflect Recent Managed Care Concerns Or Present An Opportunity
data.description:
• If you are looking at Centene and wondering whether the current share price reflects its underlying value, this article will walk through the key numbers that matter most.
  • The stock closed at US$41.53, with a 7 day return of 9.2% decline, a 30 day return of 2.5%, a year to date return of 0.6% decline, and a 1 year return of 35.8% decline, which may change how you think about both risk and upside.
  • Recent news around Centene has focused on its position in managed care and how investors are reassessing companies in the broader healthcare space. This context helps explain why the share price has moved, as the market reacts to shifting expectations for health insurers and reimbursement trends.
  • Against that backdrop, Centene currently has a valuation score of 5/6, reflecting where it screens as undervalued across several checks. Next we will look at how different valuation approaches line up for Centene, and then finish with a way of thinking about valuation that goes beyond any single model.

Find out why Centene's -35.8% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Approach 1: Centene Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes the cash Centene is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those projections back into today’s dollars to estimate what the business might be worth right now.

Centene’s latest twelve month free cash flow is about $3.24b. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts and extrapolated estimates project free cash flow reaching $4.04b by 2030, with interim yearly projections between 2026 and 2035 discounted back to today. Simply Wall St uses analyst inputs for the earlier years and then extends the trend beyond the typical 5 year forecast window.

When all of those discounted cash flows are added up, the model points to an estimated intrinsic value of about $214.03 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $41.53, this implies the stock appears around 80.6% undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Centene Price vs Sales

For profitable companies that generate meaningful revenue, the P/S ratio is a straightforward way to connect what you are paying for each share with the sales the business produces. It is especially useful when earnings are affected by accounting items or near term factors that make P/E less informative.

What investors usually see as a “normal” or “fair” P/S depends on what they expect for future growth and how risky they believe the business is. Higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher uncertainty often line up with a lower one.

Centene currently trades on a P/S ratio of 0.12x, compared with a Healthcare industry average of 1.27x and a peer average of 1.57x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework estimates a P/S of 0.88x for Centene, based on factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics.

This Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the sector because it adjusts for Centene’s own growth outlook, risk and profitability rather than assuming it should trade in line with a broad average. Since the current 0.12x P/S is well below the 0.88x Fair Ratio, the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit inside the Community page, so you can quickly plug in your assumptions and see how your fair value for Centene compares with the current share price, which may help you decide whether it looks attractive or stretched. data.title: Is InMode Ltd.'s (NASDAQ:INMD) Recent Stock Performance Tethered To Its Strong Fundamentals?
data.description:
InMode (NASDAQ:INMD) has had a great run on the share market with its stock up by a significant 10% over the last week. Since the market usually pay for a company’s long-term fundamentals, we decided to study the company’s key performance indicators to see if they could be influencing the market. Particularly, we will be paying attention to InMode's ROE today.

Return on equity or ROE is a key measure used to assess how efficiently a company's management is utilizing the company's capital. In short, ROE shows the profit each dollar generates with respect to its shareholder investments.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

How To Calculate Return On Equity?

ROE can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for InMode is:

23% = US$150m ÷ US$653m (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' refers to a company's earnings over the last year. Another way to think of that is that for every $1 worth of equity, the company was able to earn $0.23 in profit.

See our latest analysis for InMode

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

InMode's Earnings Growth And 23% ROE

To start with, InMode's ROE looks acceptable. Further, the company's ROE compares quite favorably to the industry average of 9.8%. This certainly adds some context to InMode's decent 10.0% net income growth seen over the past five years.

As a next step, we compared InMode's net income growth with the industry and found that the company has a similar growth figure when compared with the industry average growth rate of 12% in the same period.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. By doing so, they will have an idea if the stock is headed into clear blue waters or if swampy waters await. One good indicator of expected earnings growth is the P/E ratio which determines the price the market is willing to pay for a stock based on its earnings prospects. So, you may want to check if InMode is trading on a high P/E or a low P/E, relative to its industry.

Is InMode Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

InMode doesn't pay any regular dividends, meaning that all of its profits are being reinvested in the business, which explains the fair bit of earnings growth the company has seen.

Summary

Overall, we are quite pleased with InMode's performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. That being so, according to the latest industry analyst forecasts, the company's earnings are expected to shrink in the future.
